summary: McKenzie outlines his development plan for launching Bingo Card Creator, his first software product. He prioritizes shipping a minimum viable product by deferring features like Print Preview and document templates to version 2.0. The plan covers finishing core printing code, building the website with SEO-optimized content, and setting up advertising through Google AdWords and eBay. He estimates 15 hours of work remaining, targeting a launch by the end of the week.
key_points: Deliberately defers nice-to-have features (Print Preview, templates) to v2.0 in favor of shipping sooner and getting feedback
Website content strategy includes 'SEO bait' sample lesson plans alongside standard product pages
Marketing channels planned from day one: Google AdWords, eBay, Payloadz, and eSellerate
Demonstrates disciplined time estimation and scope management for a solo developer launch
money_quotes: they're wonderful features for my customers but the program is usable and worthwhile without them, and the sooner I can put this out on the Internet the sooner I can start getting feedback
Yay for projects which stay on schedule
key_insight: Ship the minimum viable product quickly to start getting feedback, deferring polish and nice-to-have features to future versions.
